steps.title: Spot the dissociation we need
steps.content: The prediction was that positive feedback would produce two effects: psychological (enhanced enjoyment) and physical (improved endurance). The question asks for a finding where this prediction partially fails—where positive feedback worked psychologically but not physically. We're looking for: greater enjoyment but similar (not improved) exercise duration.

steps.title: Match the dissociation
steps.content: Choice A directly shows this dissociation: participants receiving positive feedback reported greater enjoyment but maintained their heart and pedaling rates for approximately the same duration as those receiving negative or neutral feedback. The other three choices either highlight different feedback groups, obscure positive feedback's specific effects, or suggest positive feedback actually did improve physical endurance. That matches choice A — A is correct.
